# Skill: sense-conventions

# Check conventions with Sense

Use Sense conventions to understand project patterns before writing new code.

## Steps

1. Run `sense_conventions` to see all detected patterns.
2. If working in a specific domain, run `sense_conventions domain="<domain>"` to filter.
3. Follow the detected patterns in your new code — match naming conventions, file structure, testing patterns, and inheritance hierarchies.
4. If you need to deviate from a convention, note why.

<skill_content name="sense-explore">
# Skill: sense-explore

# Explore codebase with Sense

Use Sense MCP tools to navigate the codebase structurally.

## Steps

1. Run `sense_status` to confirm the index is healthy.
2. Run `sense_search query="<topic>"` to find relevant symbols.
3. For each interesting symbol, run `sense_graph symbol="<name>"` to see callers, callees, and relationships.
4. If a symbol has many connections, run `sense_graph symbol="<name>" direction="callers"` or `sense_graph symbol="<name>" direction="callees"` to focus.
5. Summarize the architecture you found: key symbols, how they connect, entry points.

<skill_content name="sense-impact">
# Skill: sense-impact

# Impact analysis with Sense

Use Sense blast radius to understand what a change will affect before making it.

## Steps

1. Run `sense_blast symbol="<name>"` to see direct and indirect callers.
2. Review the affected files and symbols — pay attention to test coverage.
3. If the blast radius is large, consider whether to change the interface or add a new symbol instead.
4. After making changes, run `sense_blast diff="HEAD~1"` to verify the actual scope matches expectations.

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module OutOfOffisable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   OFFISABLE_ATTRS = %w[day_of_week closed_all_day open_hour open_minutes close_hour close_minutes open_all_day].freeze
7: 
8:   included do
9:     has_many :working_hours, dependent: :destroy_async
10:     after_create :create_default_working_hours
11:   end
12: 
13:   def out_of_office?
14:     working_hours_enabled? && working_hours.today.closed_now?
15:   end
16: 
17:   def working_now?
18:     !out_of_office?
19:   end
20: 
21:   def weekly_schedule
22:     working_hours.sort_by(&:day_of_week).map do |wh|
23:       {
24:         'day_of_week' => wh.day_of_week,
25:         'closed_all_day' => wh.closed_all_day,
26:         'open_hour' => wh.open_hour,
27:         'open_minutes' => wh.open_minutes,
28:         'close_hour' => wh.close_hour,
29:         'close_minutes' => wh.close_minutes,
30:         'open_all_day' => wh.open_all_day
31:       }
32:     end
33:   end
34: 
35:   # accepts an array of hashes similiar to the format of weekly_schedule
36:   #  [
37:   #    { "day_of_week"=>1,
38:   #      "closed_all_day"=>false,
39:   #      "open_hour"=>9,
40:   #      "open_minutes"=>0,
41:   #      "close_hour"=>17,
42:   #      "close_minutes"=>0,
43:   #      "open_all_day=>false" },...]
44:   def update_working_hours(params)
45:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
46:       params.each do |working_hour|
47:         working_hours.find_by(day_of_week: working_hour['day_of_week']).update(working_hour.slice(*OFFISABLE_ATTRS))
48:       end
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   private

28:     self.class.columns.each do |column|

46:     errors.add(column.name.to_sym, "is too long (maximum is #{max_length} characters)")

1: class DeleteObjectJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :low
3: 
4:   BATCH_SIZE = 5_000
5: 
6:   def perform(object, user = nil, ip = nil)
7:     # Pre-purge heavy associations for large objects to avoid
8:     # timeouts & race conditions due to destroy_async fan-out.
9:     purge_heavy_associations(object)
10:     object.destroy!
11:     process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip)
12:   end
13: 
14:   def process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip); end
15: 
16:   private
17: 
18:   def heavy_associations
19:     {
20:       Account => %i[conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events],
21:       Inbox => %i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]
22:     }.freeze
23:   end
24: 
25:   def purge_heavy_associations(object)
26:     klass = heavy_associations.keys.find { |k| object.is_a?(k) }
27:     return unless klass
28: 
29:     heavy_associations[klass].each do |assoc|
30:       next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)
31: 
32:       batch_destroy(object.public_send(assoc))
33:     end
34:   end
35: 
36:   def batch_destroy(relation)
37:     relation.find_in_batches(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
38:       batch.each(&:destroy!)
39:     end
40:   end
41: end
42: 
43: DeleteObjectJob.prepend_mod_with('DeleteObjectJob')

1: class AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   IN_FLIGHT_TTL = 5.minutes
5: 
6:   # Coalesce per inbox: at most one AssignmentJob per inbox is in-flight
7:   # (queued or running) at any time. The marker carries a token so a job only
8:   # releases its own claim (a newer job may have taken it after a TTL lapse).
9:   def self.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox_id)
10:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
11:     token = SecureRandom.uuid
12:     return false unless ::Redis::Alfred.set(key, token, nx: true, ex: IN_FLIGHT_TTL)
13: 
14:     return true if perform_later(inbox_id: inbox_id, token: token)
15: 
16:     # Enqueue was halted; release our own claim so the inbox isn't gated until the TTL.
17:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
18:     false
19:   rescue StandardError
20:     # Enqueue raised after we claimed the gate; release our own claim, then re-raise.
21:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
22:     raise
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform(inbox_id:, token: nil)
26:     inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id)
27:     return unless inbox
28: 
29:     service = AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.new(inbox: inbox)
30:     assigned_count = service.perform_bulk_assignment(limit: bulk_assignment_limit)
31:     Rails.logger.info "Assigned #{assigned_count} conversations for inbox #{inbox.id}"
32:   rescue StandardError => e
33:     Rails.logger.error "Bulk assignment failed for inbox #{inbox_id}: #{e.message}"
34:     raise e if Rails.env.test?
35:   ensure
36:     release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
37:   end
38: 
39:   private
40: 
41:   # Release the in-flight marker only if we still own it. The atomic
42:   # compare-and-delete ensures a job whose TTL lapsed can't delete a newer
43:   # job's claim. Tokenless (pre-deploy) jobs never claimed a key, so skip.
44:   def release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
45:     return if token.nil?
46: 
47:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
48:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
49:   end
50: 
51:   def bulk_assignment_limit
52:     ENV.fetch('AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_BULK_LIMIT', 100).to_i
53:   end
54: end

1: require 'net/imap'
2: 
3: class Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob < MutexApplicationJob
4:   queue_as :scheduled_jobs
5: 
6:   def perform(channel, interval = 1)
7:     return unless should_fetch_email?(channel)
8: 
9:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::EMAIL_MESSAGE_MUTEX, inbox_id: channel.inbox.id)
10: 
11:     with_lock(key, 5.minutes) do
12:       process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
13:     end
14:   rescue *ExceptionList::IMAP_EXCEPTIONS => e
15:     Rails.logger.error "Authorization error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
16:   rescue IOError, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, Net::IMAP::NoResponseError, Net::IMAP::BadResponseError, Net::IMAP::InvalidResponseError,
17:          Net::IMAP::ResponseParseError, Net::IMAP::ResponseReadError, Net::IMAP::ResponseTooLargeError => e
18:     Rails.logger.error "Error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
19:   rescue LockAcquisitionError
20:     Rails.logger.error "Lock failed for #{channel.inbox.id}"
21:   rescue StandardError => e
22:     ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: channel.account).capture_exception
23:   end
24: 
25:   private
26: 
27:   def should_fetch_email?(channel)
28:     channel.imap_enabled? && !channel.reauthorization_required?
29:   end
30: 
31:   def process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
32:     inbound_emails = if channel.microsoft?
33:                        Imap::MicrosoftFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
34:                      elsif channel.google?
35:                        Imap::GoogleFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
36:                      else
37:                        Imap::FetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
38:                      end
39: 
40:     inbound_emails.each do |inbound_mail|
41:       process_mail(inbound_mail, channel)
42:     end

62:       return

1: class AccountDeletionService
2:   SOFT_DELETE_EMAIL_DOMAIN = '@chatwoot-deleted.invalid'.freeze
3: 
4:   attr_reader :account, :soft_deleted_users
5: 
6:   def initialize(account:)
7:     @account = account
8:     @soft_deleted_users = []
9:   end
10: 
11:   def perform
12:     Rails.logger.info("Deleting account #{account.id} - #{account.name} that was marked for deletion")
13: 
14:     soft_delete_orphaned_users
15:     send_compliance_notification
16:     DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def send_compliance_notification
22:     AdministratorNotifications::AccountComplianceMailer.with(
23:       account: account,
24:       soft_deleted_users: soft_deleted_users
25:     ).account_deleted(account).deliver_later
26:   end
27: 
28:   def soft_delete_orphaned_users
29:     account.users.each do |user|
30:       # Skip users who are still associated with another account.
31:       next if user.account_users.where.not(account_id: account.id).exists?
32: 
33:       original_email = user.email
34:       user.email = soft_deleted_email_for(user)
35:       user.skip_reconfirmation!
36:       user.save!
37: 
38:       user_info = {
39:         id: user.id.to_s,
40:         original_email: original_email
41:       }
42: 
43:       soft_deleted_users << user_info
44: 
45:       Rails.logger.info("Soft deleted user #{user.id} with email #{original_email}")
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def soft_deleted_email_for(user)
50:     "#{user.id}#{SOFT_DELETE_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"
51:   end

62:     changed_attributes = extract_changed_attributes(event)
63:     return if changed_attributes.blank?
64:

71:     inbox_webhook_data = Inbox::EventDataPresenter.new(inbox).webhook_data
72:     payload = inbox_webhook_data.merge(event: __method__.to_s)

80:

96:   def handle_typing_status(event_name, event)
97:     conversation = event.data[:conversation]
98:     user = event.data[:user]
99:     inbox = conversation.inbox
100: 
101:     payload = {
102:       event: event_name,
103:       user: user.webhook_data,
104:       conversation: conversation.webhook_data,
105:       is_private: event.data[:is_private] || false
106:     }
107:     deliver_webhook_payloads(payload, inbox)

110:   def deliver_account_webhooks(payload, account)
111:     account.webhooks.account_type.each do |webhook|
112:       next unless webhook.subscriptions.include?(payload[:event])
113: 
114:       WebhookJob.perform_later(webhook.url, payload, :account_webhook,
115:                                secret: webhook.secret,
116:                                delivery_id: SecureRandom.uuid)
117:     end
118:   end

121:     return unless inbox.channel_type == 'Channel::Api'
122:     return if inbox.channel.webhook_url.blank?
123: 
124:     WebhookJob.perform_later(inbox.channel.webhook_url, payload, :api_inbox_webhook,
125:                              secret: inbox.channel.secret, delivery_id: SecureRandom.uuid)
126:   end
127: 
128:   def deliver_webhook_payloads(payload, inbox)
129:     deliver_account_webhooks(payload, inbox.account)
130:     deliver_api_inbox_webhooks(payload, inbox)

7:     # FIXME: for now we will only send messages from widget to slack

10:     return if message.external_source_id_slack.present?

12:     return if invalid_message?
13: 
14:     perform_reply

17:   def link_unfurl(event)
18:     slack_client.chat_unfurl(
19:       event
20:     )
21:     # You may wonder why we're not requesting reauthorization and disabling hooks when scope errors occur.
22:     # Since link unfurling is just a nice-to-have feature that doesn't affect core functionality, we will silently ignore these errors.
23:   rescue Slack::Web::Api::Errors::MissingScope => e

29:   def valid_channel_for_slack?
30:     # slack wouldn't be an ideal interface to reply to tweets, hence disabling that case
31:     return false if channel.is_a?(Channel::TwitterProfile) && conversation.additional_attributes['type'] == 'tweet'
32: 
33:     true
34:   end

36:   def invalid_message?
37:     (message.outgoing? || message.template?) && conversation.identifier.blank?
38:   end
39: 
40:   def perform_reply
41:     send_message
42: 
43:     return unless @slack_message
44: 
45:     update_reference_id
46:     update_external_source_id_slack
47:   end
48: 
49:   def message_content
50:     private_indicator = message.private? ? 'private: ' : ''
51:     sanitized_content = ActionView::Base.full_sanitizer.sanitize(format_message_content)
52: 
53:     if conversation.identifier.present?
54:       "#{private_indicator}#{sanitized_content}"
55:     else
56:       "#{formatted_inbox_name}#{formatted_conversation_link}#{email_subject_line}\n#{sanitized_content}"
57:     end
58:   end
59: 
60:   def format_message_content
61:     message.message_type == 'activity' ? "_#{message_text}_" : message_text
62:   end
63: 
64:   def message_text
65:     content = message.processed_message_content || message.content
66: 
67:     if content.present?
68:       content.gsub(MENTION_REGEX, '\1')
69:     else
70:       content
71:     end
72:   end
73: 
74:   def formatted_inbox_name
75:     "\n*Inbox:* #{message.inbox.name} (#{message.inbox.inbox_type})\n"
76:   end
77: 
78:   def formatted_conversation_link
79:     "#{link_to_conversation} to view the conversation.\n"
80:   end
81: 
82:   def email_subject_line
83:     return '' unless message.inbox.email?
84: 
85:     email_payload = message.content_attributes['email']
86:     return "*Subject:* #{email_payload['subject']}\n\n" if email_payload.present? && email_payload['subject'].present?
87: 
88:     ''
89:   end
90: 
91:   def avatar_url(sender)
92:     sender_type = sender_type(sender).downcase
93:     blob_key = sender&.avatar&.attached? ? sender.avatar.blob.key : nil
94:     generate_url(sender_type, blob_key)
95:   end
96: 
97:   def generate_url(sender_type, blob_key)
98:     base_url = ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)
99:     "#{base_url}/slack_uploads?blob_key=#{blob_key}&sender_type=#{sender_type}"
100:   end
101: 
102:   def send_message
103:     post_message if message_content.present?
104:     upload_files if message.attachments.any?
105:   rescue Slack::Web::Api::Errors::IsArchived, Slack::Web::Api::Errors::AccountInactive, Slack::Web::Api::Errors::MissingScope,
106:          Slack::Web::Api::Errors::InvalidAuth,
107:          Slack::Web::Api::Errors::ChannelNotFound, Slack::Web::Api::Errors::NotInChannel => e
108:     Rails.logger.error e
109:     hook.prompt_reauthorization!
110:     hook.disable
111:   end
112: 
113:   def post_message
114:     @slack_message = slack_client.chat_postMessage(
115:       channel: hook.reference_id,
116:       text: message_content,
117:       username: sender_name(message.sender),
118:       thread_ts: conversation.identifier,
119:       icon_url: avatar_url(message.sender),
120:       unfurl_links: conversation.identifier.present?
121:     )
122:   end
123: 
124:   def upload_files
125:     files = build_files_array
126:     return if files.empty?
127: 
128:     begin
129:       result = slack_client.files_upload_v2(
130:         files: files,
131:         initial_comment: 'Attached File!',
132:         thread_ts: conversation.identifier,
133:         channel_id: hook.reference_id
134:       )
135:       Rails.logger.info "slack_upload_result: #{result}"
136:     rescue Slack::Web::Api::Errors::SlackError => e
137:       Rails.logger.error "Failed to upload files: #{e.message}"
138:     ensure
139:       files.each { |file| file[:content]&.clear }
140:     end
141:   end
142: 
143:   def build_files_array
144:     message.attachments.filter_map do |attachment|
145:       next unless attachment.with_attached_file?
146: 
147:       build_file_payload(attachment)
148:     end
149:   end
150: 
151:   def build_file_payload(attachment)
152:     content = download_attachment_content(attachment)
153:     return if content.blank?
154: 
155:     {
156:       filename: attachment.file.filename.to_s,
157:       content: content,
158:       title: attachment.file.filename.to_s
159:     }
160:   end
161: 
162:   def download_attachment_content(attachment)
163:     buffer = +''
164:     attachment.file.blob.open do |file|
165:       while (chunk = file.read(64.kilobytes))
166:         buffer << chunk
167:       end
168:     end
169:     buffer
170:   end
171: 
172:   def sender_name(sender)
173:     sender.try(:name) ? "#{sender.try(:name)} (#{sender_type(sender)})" : sender_type(sender)
174:   end
175: 
176:   def sender_type(sender)
177:     if sender.instance_of?(Contact)
178:       'Contact'
179:     elsif sender.instance_of?(User)
180:       'Agent'
181:     elsif message.message_type == 'activity' && sender.nil?
182:       'System'
183:     else
184:       'Bot'
185:     end
186:   end

188:   def update_reference_id
189:     return unless should_update_reference_id?
190: 
191:     conversation.update!(identifier: @slack_message['ts'])
192:   end
193: 
194:   def update_external_source_id_slack
195:     return unless @slack_message['message']
196: 
197:     message.update!(external_source_id_slack: "cw-origin-#{@slack_message['message']['ts']}")
198:   end

201:     @slack_client ||= Slack::Web::Client.new(token: hook.access_token)
202:   end

204:   def link_to_conversation
205:     "<#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/app/accounts/#{conversation.account_id}/conversations/#{conversation.display_id}|Click here>"
206:   end

211:   # - If the thread_ts is blank, it means that the conversation was previously connected in a different channel.
212:   def should_update_reference_id?
